course_description:
This course considers the nature of the law of tort. It examines intentional torts against the person, and torts in relation to land and the enjoyment of land. The course also includes an introduction to the tort of negligence (including modification by the Civil Liability Act 2003 (Qld)).

Law of Torts I introduces students to the law of torts. The law of torts consists of the law of civil (non-criminal) wrongs other than breach of contract. Law of Torts I covers the following: the nature and aims of tort law; intentional torts against the person; torts to land (trespass and nuisance);ﾠbasic elements of the law of negligence, as modified by the Civil Liability Act 2003 (Qld); damages for personal injury; and actions on death.ﾠ

course_aims:
Education Mission Statement

The mission of the School of Law is to educate you in and about the law. We seek to assist you to think critically about the law and to investigate its structures, principles, policies, and values. This is achieved by engaging with you as fellow investigators in a range of critical and intellectual conversations about the law and its place in society. Our role is thus to guide you in your own intellectual journey in the law and to equip you with the necessary skills that will enable you to continue that journey long after you have left the School.

Course Aims

The broad aims of the course are to:

learning_outcomes.description: Articulate the key concepts and principles in the law of torts.
Engage thoughtfully with case law and statutes.
Analyse facts and identify legal solutions to the problems raised by those facts.

assessment_details.title: In-semester Online Examination - Invigilated
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The examination is a closed book examination conducted via Learn.UQ during the lecture time in week 5. The examination will consist of a number of multiple choice questions totalling 40 marks. Students will need to bring with them an electronic device - iPad, Surface Pro or laptop computer which is set up and able to connect to the University's wi-fi network. Please ensure that prior to entering the examination venue, you log onto the University network (using double factor authentication) and that your device is fully charged. Students may only access the exam questions during the exam - not notes, websites or any external materials of any type. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time no planning time minutes Duration 60 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Closed book examination - no written materials permitted Exam platform Other Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The exam will be conducted via Learn.UQ.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Lectures The lecture topics are: Torts: An Overview/Intentional Torts I Intentional Torts Against the Person Problem Solvi Trespass to Land Nuisance Negligence 1- The Duty of Care Negligence 2 - Breach of Duty Negligence 3 - Causation and Remoteness of Damage Negligence 4 - Multiple Tortfeasors Negligence 5 - Defences Actions on Death Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 12 (23 Feb - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorials The tutorial topics are: Torts: An Overview Intentional Torts Against the Person Problem Solvi Intentional Torts Against the Person False Imprisonment - Case Reading Exercise Negligence Overview Negligence 2 - Breach of Duty Negligence 3 - Causation and Remoteness of Damage Negligence 4 - Multiple Tortfeasors Negligence 5 - Defences Revision Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
